purpose: Close the registry gap 'MaveDB needs a real-score scoreset' — validate the MaveDB adapter on REAL experimental (non-imputed) score sets with ESM2-35M zero-shot masked-marginals.
finding: The MaveDB adapter shows real ESM signal on clean single-missense DMS (PTEN VAMP-seq 0.281, NUDT15 stability 0.341 vs ~0 shuffled). Multi-mutant libraries (UBE2I BarSeq/TileSeq/joint) give chance on their small/noisy single-missense subset — this is a data-design property (the raw library scores combinations), NOT an adapter bug (mism=0 throughout). ProteinGym's value-add is precisely the curation into clean single-missense subsets with joint/imputed scores.
